commands.review_summary:
The PR recomputes known-priced zero-cost transcript entries in session cost summaries, cache rebuilds, and session logs, bumps the usage-cost cache version, and adds DeepSeek V4 regression coverage.

PR surface: Source +23, Tests +68. Total +91 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for a source-level reproduction: current main parses cost.total 0 into costBreakdown and only estimates flat pricing when costTotal is undefined, so known-priced nonzero usage can remain zero. I did not run a live DeepSeek call or tests in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review should wait for contributor after-fix real behavior proof and decide whether the cache-version upgrade evidence is sufficient; there is no narrow automated code repair left.

target.title: [codex] Estimate known zero transcript costs
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes #97047.

DeepSeek V4 compatible API transcripts can record `usage.cost.total` as `0` even when token usage is present. OpenClaw trusted that zero, so Control UI Spend and session summaries could show known-priced DeepSeek V4 usage as free.

## Why This Change Was Made

`src/infra/session-cost-usage.ts` now treats a recorded zero total as stale when the model has known pricing and non-zero token usage, then recomputes the estimate through the existing pricing path. The cost cache version was bumped so stale zero-cost summaries are refreshed, while unknown-pricing zero totals still remain missing-cost entries.

## User Impact

Spend totals and session logs now show an estimated cost for known-priced DeepSeek V4 usage instead of silently reporting zero. Existing cached summaries are refreshed by the version bump.

## Evidence

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/session-cost-usage.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
target.author: nianjiuzst
